definition: The Kalecki-Levy profit identity (Profits = Investment + Government Deficit + Net Exports - Household Saving + Dividends) is an accounting tautology that holds by construction. The empirical relationship between deficit changes and profit changes is statistically significant (R² ~0.55-0.65, Granger causality confirmed) but explains only roughly half of profit variance. The channel is real and directionally important but not the near-mechanical guarantee implied by 9/10 confidence.
evidence: R² of 0.55-0.65 on annual NIPA data 1947-2024
Granger causality: deficit changes predict profit changes (t-stat 3.2-4.8), not reverse
VAR impulse response: +0.4-0.7pp GDP profit increase per 1pp GDP deficit shock
Rolling regression shows channel strengthening post-2000
Japan and 1990s U.S. surplus as counterexamples to naive causal interpretation
Coefficient sensitive to recession inclusion — drops to 0.3-0.4 excluding recessions
open_questions: Does rising interest expense composition degrade the profit-sustaining channel?
What is the correct fiscal multiplier for the Kalecki decomposition at current debt levels?
Can the channel persist when deficits are dominated by interest payments rather than productive spending?
What would constitute falsifying evidence against the Kalecki-dominance thesis?
